title: Specialization is the New Frontier: Moving beyond General Purpose
summary: As foundational models converge in capability, the real differentiation is emerging in specialized variants. General purpose AI is becoming a commodity, and value is concentrating in domain-specific expertise.
content:
For years, the holy grail of artificial intelligence has been AGI—Artificial General Intelligence. The prevailing logic was that a single, massive model trained on all human knowledge would eventually be capable of solving any problem. While the pursuit of a unified "god model" continues, the commercial reality of March 2026 points in a very different direction: extreme specialization.

The Commoditization of the Generalist

We are reaching a point where "general intelligence" is becoming a commodity. The baseline capabilities of writing an email, summarizing a document, or writing a Python script are now available in dozens of models, both open-source and proprietary. When everyone has access to a brilliant intern, the intern ceases to be a competitive advantage.

This convergence forces AI labs to differentiate not by being generally smarter, but by being specifically expert. A model that is 99% accurate on a general knowledge test is impressive, but a model that is 99% accurate at diagnosing anomalous patterns in a genomic sequence is transformative.

The Rise of Domain Experts

We are seeing the rollout of models trained explicitly for high-value verticals. Instead of one massive GPT or Claude, we are getting variants optimized for cybersecurity, financial modeling, or life sciences. These models don't just know the vocabulary of the field; they have been fine-tuned on proprietary data and engineered to reason within the specific constraints of that domain.

This shift from generalist to specialist mirrors human expertise. You don't ask your general practitioner to perform neurosurgery. Why would we expect a model trained on Reddit and Wikipedia to accurately audit a complex smart contract?

The Future is a Committee

The architecture of the near future isn't a single, monolithic brain. It's a routing system that takes a complex user query and delegates it to a committee of specialized experts. The "general" model acts merely as the receptionist, parsing intent and calling the relevant specialist agent. The frontier of AI is no longer just scale; it is deep, uncompromising focus.

Contributor: Alessandro Linzi
